name: Camera Zoom
description: A camera behavior that changes field of view or orthographic scale to show more detail or more surrounding space.
design_notes: Camera zoom should have a clear owner: player input, context, targeting, inspection, or cinematic framing. The important design choice is whether zoom changes decision-making or only presentation. Fast zoom can help urgency but can also cause motion discomfort, while slow zoom can hide threats if it lags behind gameplay state.
design_purpose: Let players inspect distant details or widen context without moving the avatar.
Frame aiming, map reading, building, or tactical planning at the right information scale.
Support cinematic emphasis while preserving orientation and UI readability.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to pull the view closer for precision or back out to understand the surrounding space.

parameters.name: min_zoom_scale
parameters.description: Closest allowed zoom for orthographic scale or equivalent camera distance.

parameters.name: max_zoom_scale
parameters.description: Farthest allowed zoom for orthographic scale or equivalent camera distance.

parameters.name: zoom_blend_ms
parameters.description: Blend duration used when changing zoom targets.

parameters.name: zoom_step_ratio
parameters.description: Multiplier applied per scroll wheel, trigger, or button step.

parameters.name: zoom_collision_padding
parameters.description: Clearance used to keep zoomed cameras from clipping into geometry.

edge_cases: Zooming into walls or ceilings should shorten distance without pushing the camera through collision.
UI scale and reticle placement must remain readable at both minimum and maximum zoom.
Cutscenes, lock-on, photo mode, and dialogue can all request zoom; priority and restore rules must be explicit.
Changing aspect ratio or split-screen layout should recompute zoom bounds instead of reusing unsafe values.
common_bugs: Zoom target is applied to FOV and camera distance at the same time, doubling the perceived effect.
Camera collision runs before zoom interpolation and causes popping in tight spaces.
Manual zoom is lost after respawn because default camera state overwrites player preference.
Mouse-wheel zoom steps vary by platform and make tuning inconsistent.
balancing_notes: Wide zoom improves awareness but can reduce tension, surprise, or precision reading of nearby hazards.
Aim or inspection zoom should usually trade awareness, movement, or input commitment for the extra detail it grants.
accessibility_notes: Provide zoom speed controls or instant-step options for players sensitive to animated camera scaling.
Avoid using zoom alone as critical feedback; pair it with stable UI, sound, or object highlighting.

implementation_notes.unity: With Cinemachine, drive Lens.FieldOfView or OrthographicSize from a zoom target and apply camera collision after interpolation; with a custom rig, separate zoom distance from framing offset.
implementation_notes.godot: For Camera2D, interpolate the zoom vector; for Camera3D, interpolate fov or boom distance and run collision checks before committing the final transform.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use PlayerCameraManager, SpringArm TargetArmLength, or camera component FOV interpolation, and clamp zoom requests through a shared camera mode stack.
implementation_notes.web: For Canvas or Phaser, update camera scale around a stable focus point; for Three.js, interpolate FOV or camera distance and update projection matrices after changes.

example_games.title: Factorio
example_games.note: Uses player-controlled zoom to inspect factory detail or view broader layouts.

example_games.title: The Sims 4
example_games.note: Uses camera zoom as a core navigation and inspection control.
